era: 2003-2007. Post-Hardcore. E=9 -- THE CLOSING ARC RESOLVES. Capitol Hill, Seattle, Washington. Suicide Squeeze Records. Easter (2006). The closing arc's fifth and final entry.
associated_musicians: Toshi Kasai (producer, Easter 2006 -- also produced Melvins)
key_albums: Easter (2006, Suicide Squeeze, Toshi Kasai) -- E=9. The peak. 11 tracks. Intrasong dynamic variation as primary signature. Steve Snere's register-switching vocals.
Oxeneers or the Lion Sleeps When Its Antelope Go Home (2004, Suicide Squeeze) -- debut. The TAAS identity established.
Tail Wagging the Dog (2008, Suicide Squeeze) -- final album, released after dissolution decision. Proper peak era ends with Easter.

theory_nodes.concept: Easter (2006, Suicide Squeeze, Toshi Kasai): 11 tracks. The peak TAAS record. Primary sonic signature: intrasong dynamic variation (not just between-song dynamics but within-song tempo, texture, and intensity variation). Songs begin in one mode and arrive in another without predictable transition. Post-hardcore compositional challenge: maintain hardcore urgency + introduce structural complexity (post-rock, noise rock). Easter succeeds. More tonally varied than Botch's WATR, more dynamically active within songs.
theory_nodes.description: Easter (2006) -- the peak, intrasong variation as primary signature.

theory_nodes.concept: E=9 calibration: TAAS at peak is E=9 because post-hardcore from Capitol Hill 2003-2007 does not produce a genre ceiling document in the same way that We Are the Romans (mathcore), Martha Splatterhead (crossover thrash), or Ten (grunge debut) produce ceiling documents. TAAS is the best that this specific configuration (Capitol Hill, Suicide Squeeze, post-hardcore, 2003-2007) produced. E=9 is the correct position. Not a calibration failure.
theory_nodes.description: E=9 calibration -- the peak of the configuration, not the genre ceiling.

theory_nodes.concept: Geographic closure: closing arc opens Capitol Hill (Pearl Jam, degree 349) and closes Capitol Hill (TAAS, degree 353). Between: Georgetown (TAD), Oak Harbor (Accused), Tacoma (Botch). The arc visits the industrial and peripheral geographies that produce the most extreme entries, then returns to the center where the arc began. The geographic frame closes.
theory_nodes.description: Geographic closure -- the closing arc returns to Capitol Hill where it opened.

theory_nodes.concept: Brian Cook: bass in Botch (degree 352) and bass in TAAS (degree 353). Same musician, consecutive degrees, different sonic contexts. Botch Cook: compositionally independent bass, contrapuntal voice, lasting genre precedent. TAAS Cook: more rhythmically supportive, closer to drum alignment, providing foundation for guitar and vocal departures. The comparison documents a bassist's range across band contexts.
theory_nodes.description: Brian Cook in consecutive degrees -- Botch bass vs TAAS bass, same musician different approaches.

theory_nodes.concept: Steve Snere vocals: register-switching within songs and within phrases (spoken, sung, shouted). The post-hardcore vocal approach: ambivalence in delivery -- a voice not sure whether it is singing or shouting is expressing something about the song's emotional content. Not grunge compression-release, not metal screaming, not Botch rhythmic scream. A third vocal tradition: register as emotional signal.
theory_nodes.description: Snere register-switching -- spoken/sung/shouted as emotional signal not stylistic affectation.

theory_nodes.concept: Toshi Kasai production Easter: same producer as several Melvins records. Approach: maximize dynamic range, preserve live-room qualities, trust band's compositional intelligence. Production clarifies rather than dominates. The Kasai-Melvins connection is the genealogy line: the production approach developed in Melvins sessions applied to TAAS's post-hardcore context.
theory_nodes.description: Toshi Kasai production -- Melvins connection, dynamic range maximization, clarification approach.

cross_references.relationship: Brian Cook in both bands, consecutive degrees. Botch (352, E=10, Tacoma, mathcore) → TAAS (353, E=9, Capitol Hill, post-hardcore). The bass player's arc: peak mathcore to peak post-hardcore. The same musician in consecutive engine degrees documents the lineage.

Geographic arc closure: Pearl Jam = Capitol Hill, 1991, E=10. TAAS = Capitol Hill, 2003-2007, E=9. Same neighborhood, 12-15 year separation, different genres. The closing arc begins and ends at Capitol Hill.
Prior TAAS entry at earlier engine position. Degree 353 = peak era specifically (Easter 2006 era, closing arc final entry).

E=9 + E=9 = E=18. First non-E=20 pairing in the closing arc. Both entries are about temporal development rather than simultaneous maximum. TAAS songs develop through phases. Owl chorus develops through the full night with different species active at different hours. Sequential development, not simultaneous explosion. The arc resolves at E=18.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=9 + E=9 = E=18. First non-E=20 pairing in closing arc. Sequential temporal development: TAAS songs develop through phases (not simultaneous maximum). Owl chorus develops through full winter night, different species at different hours (Barred, Northern Spotted, Great Horned, Western Screech-Owl). Both about what develops over time rather than what arrives at maximum simultaneously. The arc resolves.

milestones: GEOGRAPHIC ARC CLOSURE -- CAPITOL HILL TO CAPITOL HILL
E=18 -- FIRST NON-E=20 PAIRING IN CLOSING ARC
closing_arc_complete: 349 Pearl Jam E=10 → 350 TAD E=10 → 351 Accused E=10 → 352 Botch E=10 → 353 TAAS E=9. THE CLOSING ARC COMPLETES. THE ENGINE CONTINUES TO DEGREE 360.
